data.content:
### Supabase Project Configuration File Structure (config.toml)

Source: https://context7.com/supabase/cli/llms.txt

This TOML snippet illustrates the complete structure of the `config.toml` file used to define settings for local and remote Supabase projects. It covers configurations for API, database, connection pooler, migrations, seed data, realtime, studio, storage (including buckets), and authentication (including external providers and email settings).

```toml
# Project identifier
project_id = "my-project-123"

# API Configuration
[api]
enabled = true
port = 54321
# Public schemas exposed via PostgREST
schemas = ["public", "graphql_public"]
# Extra search path for API requests
extra_search_path = ["public", "extensions"]
# Maximum rows returned by API
max_rows = 1000

# Database Configuration
[db]
port = 54322
major_version = 17

# Connection pooler
[db.pooler]
enabled = false
# Modes: transaction, session, statement
pool_mode = "transaction"
default_pool_size = 20
max_client_conn = 100

# Migration settings
[db.migrations]
enabled = true
# Additional schema paths to include
schema_paths = []

# Seed data
[db.seed]
enabled = true
sql_paths = ["./seed.sql"]

# Realtime Configuration
[realtime]
enabled = true
ip_version = "IPv4"

# Studio Configuration
[studio]
enabled = true
port = 54323
api_url = "http://localhost"
openai_api_key = "env(OPENAI_API_KEY)"

# Storage Configuration
[storage]
enabled = true
file_size_limit = "50MiB"
image_transformation_enabled = true

# Storage buckets
[storage.buckets.avatars]
public = true
file_size_limit = "5MiB"
allowed_mime_types = ["image/png", "image/jpeg", "image/gif", "image/webp"]

[storage.buckets.documents]
public = false
file_size_limit = "100MiB"
allowed_mime_types = ["application/pdf", "text/plain", "application/msword"]

# Auth Configuration
[auth]
enabled = true
site_url = "http://localhost:3000"
additional_redirect_urls = ["https://localhost:3000"]
jwt_expiry = 3600
enable_refresh_token_rotation = true
refresh_token_reuse_interval = 10
enable_signup = true
enable_anonymous_sign_ins = false

# External OAuth providers
[auth.external.github]
enabled = true
client_id = "env(GITHUB_CLIENT_ID)"
secret = "env(GITHUB_SECRET)"
redirect_uri = "http://localhost:54321/auth/v1/callback"

[auth.external.google]
enabled = true
client_id = "env(GOOGLE_CLIENT_ID)"
secret = "env(GOOGLE_SECRET)"

# Email auth
[auth.email]
enable_signup = true
double_confirm_changes = true
enable_confirmations = false
secure_password_change = false
```

--------------------------------

### Serve Supabase Edge Functions Locally with CLI

Source: https://context7.com/supabase/cli/llms.txt

These commands allow developers to run Supabase Edge Functions locally for development and testing purposes. Options include serving all functions, specifying environment files, custom import maps, or enabling debugging with inspect flags. It also includes an example curl command for local testing.

```bash
supabase functions serve
```

```bash
supabase functions serve --env-file .env.local
```

```bash
supabase functions serve --import-map=supabase/functions/import_map.json
```

```bash
supabase functions serve --inspect --inspect-mode=brk
```

```bash
curl -i --location --request POST 'http://localhost:54321/functions/v1/hello-world' \
  --header 'Authorization: Bearer eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9...' \
  --header 'Content-Type: application/json' \
  --data '{"name":"Functions"}'
```

--------------------------------

### Reset Supabase Local Database

Source: https://context7.com/supabase/cli/llms.txt

Resets the local database to a clean state and reapplies all migrations from scratch, which is useful for testing purposes. It supports resetting to a specific migration version and can skip the execution of the `seed.sql` file.

```bash
supabase db reset
supabase db reset --version 20240115120000
supabase db reset --no-seed
```

--------------------------------

### Test Supabase Database with pgTAP

Source: https://context7.com/supabase/cli/llms.txt

Runs pgTAP tests against the database, supporting execution of all tests or specific test files, including against a linked database. The command also provides functionality to create new test files from a pgTAP template for easier test setup.

```bash
supabase test db
supabase test db supabase/tests/users_test.sql
supabase test db --linked supabase/tests/*.sql
supabase test new test_users --template pgtap
```

```sql
BEGIN;
SELECT plan(3);

SELECT has_table('public', 'users');
SELECT has_column('public', 'users', 'email');
SELECT col_is_unique('public', 'users', 'email');

SELECT * FROM finish();
ROLLBACK;
```

--------------------------------

### Generate Bearer JWT for Testing Authenticated Endpoints

Source: https://context7.com/supabase/cli/llms.txt

Commands to generate JWT bearer tokens using the Supabase CLI for testing authenticated endpoints. Supports specifying roles, custom claims, and expiration times, with an example of testing with `curl`.

```bash
# Generate token with specific role
supabase gen bearer-jwt --role=authenticated --exp=3600

# Output: eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9...

# Generate with custom claims
supabase gen bearer-jwt --role=service_role --payload='{"user_id":"123","admin":true}'

# Generate with specific expiration
supabase gen bearer-jwt --role=authenticated --valid-for=7200

# Test with curl:
curl -H "Authorization: Bearer eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9..." \
  http://localhost:54321/rest/v1/users
```

--------------------------------

### Link Local Supabase Project to Remote (CLI)

Source: https://context7.com/supabase/cli/llms.txt

Links your local Supabase development environment to a remote project, enabling schema pulling and migration pushing. This requires prior authentication via `supabase login` and can be done interactively or by providing a database password. An option to skip the connection pooler is also available.

```bash
supabase login

supabase link --project-ref zeoxvqpvpyrxygmmatng

supabase link --project-ref zeoxvqpvpyrxygmmatng --password "your-db-password"

supabase link --project-ref zeoxvqpvpyrxygmmatng --skip-pooler
```

--------------------------------

### Lint Supabase Database Schema

Source: https://context7.com/supabase/cli/llms.txt

Checks the local database schema for common issues and typing errors. You can specify a minimum error level and configure the command to fail on specific error severities. The output highlights issues like missing indexes, primary key absence, security policy problems, and type inconsistencies.

```bash
supabase db lint --local
supabase db lint --local --level warning
supabase db lint --local --fail-on error
```

--------------------------------

### Initialize Local Supabase Project (CLI)

Source: https://context7.com/supabase/cli/llms.txt

Initializes a new Supabase project in the current directory, creating configuration files and the default folder structure. It supports various initialization options, including integration with VS Code or IntelliJ settings, and allows forcing initialization even if a configuration already exists.

```bash
supabase init

supabase init --with-vscode-settings

supabase init --with-intellij-settings

supabase init --force
```

--------------------------------

### Apply Supabase Database Migrations using Go API

Source: https://context7.com/supabase/cli/llms.txt

This Go program demonstrates how to connect to a Supabase database, list local and remote migrations, identify pending migrations, and apply them programmatically. It requires database connection details via `SUPABASE_DB_URL` environment variable and local migration files.

```go
package main

import (
    "context"
    "fmt"
    "os"
    "github.com/supabase/cli/pkg/migration"
    "github.com/supabase/cli/pkg/pgxv5"
)

func main() {
    ctx := context.Background()

    // Connect to database
    dbURL := os.Getenv("SUPABASE_DB_URL")
    conn, err := pgxv5.Connect(ctx, dbURL)
    if err != nil {
        panic(err)
    }
    defer conn.Close(ctx)

    // List local migrations
    fsys := os.DirFS(".")
    migrations, err := migration.ListLocalMigrations("supabase/migrations", fsys)
    if err != nil {
        panic(err)
    }

    fmt.Printf("Found %d migrations\n", len(migrations))

    // Get remote migration history
    remoteMigrations, err := migration.ListRemoteMigrations(ctx, conn)
    if err != nil {
        panic(err)
    }

    // Find pending migrations
    pending, err := migration.FindPendingMigrations(migrations, remoteMigrations)
    if err != nil {
        panic(err)
    }

    if len(pending) == 0 {
        fmt.Println("No pending migrations")
        return
    }

    // Apply pending migrations
    fmt.Printf("Applying %d pending migrations...\n", len(pending))
    err = migration.ApplyMigrations(ctx, pending, conn, fsys)
    if err != nil {
        panic(err)
    }

    fmt.Println("Migrations applied successfully")
}
```

--------------------------------

### List Supabase Database Migrations (CLI)

Source: https://context7.com/supabase/cli/llms.txt

Displays a list of all local and remote database migrations along with their application status. This command helps developers track which migrations have been applied and identify any discrepancies between local and linked remote projects.

```bash
supabase migration list --local

supabase migration list --linked
```

--------------------------------

### Start Local Supabase Services (CLI)

Source: https://context7.com/supabase/cli/llms.txt

Starts all Supabase services locally using Docker containers, including PostgreSQL, PostgREST, Auth, Storage, and Studio. Users can choose to exclude specific services or bypass health checks during startup. The command outputs essential connection details and API keys for local development.

```bash
supabase start

supabase start --exclude storage,imgproxy

supabase start --ignore-health-check
```

--------------------------------

### Push Supabase Migrations to Remote

Source: https://context7.com/supabase/cli/llms.txt

Applies local migrations to the remote database. This command supports dry runs to preview changes and can optionally include roles or seed data. The output provides feedback on each migration's application and any errors encountered.

```bash
supabase db push --dry-run
supabase db push
supabase db push --include-roles
supabase db push --include-seed
```

--------------------------------

### Apply or Revert Supabase Database Migrations Locally (CLI)

Source: https://context7.com/supabase/cli/llms.txt

Applies pending database migrations to the local Supabase instance or reverts previously applied migrations. Users can apply all pending migrations, optionally including seed data, or revert a specified number of the most recent migrations. The command provides output detailing the applied migrations and any encountered errors.

```bash
supabase migration up

supabase migration up --include-all

supabase migration down --last 1

supabase migration down --last 3
```

--------------------------------

### Generate Supabase TypeScript Types from PostgreSQL Schema

Source: https://context7.com/supabase/cli/llms.txt

These commands generate TypeScript types directly from a PostgreSQL database schema, either locally or from a linked Supabase project. This enables type-safe database queries, improving code reliability and developer experience by catching potential errors at compile-time. Users can also specify which schemas to generate types from.

```bash
supabase gen types --local > types/database.ts
```

```bash
supabase gen types --linked > types/supabase.ts
```

```bash
supabase gen types --local --schema=public,auth > types/database.ts
```

```typescript
export type Json = string | number | boolean | null | { [key: string]: Json } | Json[]

```

--------------------------------

### Check Supabase Service Status (CLI)

Source: https://context7.com/supabase/cli/llms.txt

Displays the status of all running local Supabase services, providing connection details and API keys. The output can be formatted as a pretty human-readable table, environment variables for easy integration, or JSON for programmatic parsing.

```bash
supabase status

supabase status -o env
# Output:
# API_URL="http://localhost:54321"
# DB_URL="postgresql://postgres:postgres@localhost:54322/postgres"
# STUDIO_URL="http://localhost:54323"
# ANON_KEY="eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9..."
# SERVICE_ROLE_KEY="eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9..."

supabase status -o json | jq '.studio_url'
```

--------------------------------

### Generate Supabase Schema Diff

Source: https://context7.com/supabase/cli/llms.txt

Compares the local database state against migration files or a remote database to generate new migration files. It supports diffing specific schemas and leveraging different diffing tools like Migra or pgAdmin.

```bash
supabase db diff --local -f new_changes
supabase db diff --linked -f sync_remote
supabase db diff --local --schema public,auth -f schema_changes
supabase db diff --use-migra --local -f changes
supabase db diff --use-pgadmin --linked -f remote_diff
```

--------------------------------

### Deploy Supabase Edge Functions using CLI

Source: https://context7.com/supabase/cli/llms.txt

This section details commands for deploying Edge Functions to a remote Supabase project. Users can deploy all functions, specific functions, or use advanced options like skipping JWT verification (for testing), custom import maps, parallel deployments, and pruning unused functions. The CLI provides a URL upon successful deployment.

```bash
supabase functions deploy
```

```bash
supabase functions deploy hello-world
```

```bash
supabase functions deploy hello-world --no-verify-jwt
```

```bash
supabase functions deploy hello-world --import-map=functions/import_map.json
```

```bash
supabase functions deploy --jobs=4
```

```bash
supabase functions deploy --prune
```

--------------------------------

### Generate Go Types from PostgreSQL Schema

Source: https://context7.com/supabase/cli/llms.txt

Commands to generate Go type definitions from a PostgreSQL schema using the Supabase CLI. Supports generation from local or linked projects and specific schemas, with an example of the resulting Go struct.

```bash
# Generate Go types from local database
supabase gen types --lang=go --local > types/database.go

# Generate from linked project
supabase gen types --lang=go --linked > types/supabase.go

# Generate specific schemas
supabase gen types --lang=go --local --schema=public > types/public.go
```

```go
# Generated Go types example:
package types

import "time"

type User struct {
    ID        string    `json:"id"`
    Email     string    `json:"email"`
    CreatedAt time.Time `json:"created_at"`
}
```

--------------------------------

### Supabase CLI Logout

Source: https://context7.com/supabase/cli/llms.txt

This command removes the local authentication credentials for the Supabase CLI, effectively logging the user out. After logging out, users must re-authenticate to access and manage remote Supabase projects.

```bash
supabase logout
```

--------------------------------

### Create New Supabase Edge Function using CLI

Source: https://context7.com/supabase/cli/llms.txt

This command initializes a new Supabase Edge Function with boilerplate code. It creates a new file in the `supabase/functions/` directory containing a basic Deno/TypeScript serverless function structure, ready for customization and development.

```bash
supabase functions new hello-world
```

```typescript
import { serve } from "https://deno.land/std@0.168.0/http/server.ts"

serve(async (req) => {
  const { name } = await req.json()
  const data = {
    message: `Hello ${name}!`,
  }

  return new Response(
    JSON.stringify(data),
    { headers: { "Content-Type": "application/json" } },
  )
})
```

--------------------------------

### Authenticate Supabase CLI Login

Source: https://context7.com/supabase/cli/llms.txt

This command facilitates authentication with Supabase, allowing users to log in either interactively through a browser or directly by piping an access token. Upon successful authentication, the token is saved locally to enable subsequent CLI operations.

```bash
supabase login

echo "sbp_xxx..." | supabase login
```

--------------------------------

### Generate Swift Types from PostgreSQL Schema

Source: https://context7.com/supabase/cli/llms.txt

Commands to generate Swift type definitions from a PostgreSQL schema for iOS development using the Supabase CLI. Includes options for access control (public/internal) and an example of the generated Swift struct.

```bash
# Generate Swift types with public access
supabase gen types --lang=swift --local --swift-access-control=public > Types.swift

# Generate with internal access (default)
supabase gen types --lang=swift --linked --swift-access-control=internal > Database.swift
```

```swift
# Generated Swift types example:
import Foundation

public struct User: Codable, Hashable, Sendable {
    public let id: UUID
    public let email: String
    public let createdAt: Date

    enum CodingKeys: String, CodingKey {
        case id
        case email
        case createdAt = "created_at"
    }
}
```

--------------------------------

### Download Supabase Edge Function Source Code with CLI

Source: https://context7.com/supabase/cli/llms.txt

This command facilitates downloading the source code of a specific Edge Function from the remote Supabase project. It's useful for synchronizing function definitions across different development environments or for backup purposes.

```bash
supabase functions download hello-world
```

--------------------------------

### Seed Supabase Storage Buckets using Go API

Source: https://context7.com/supabase/cli/llms.txt

This Go program shows how to programmatically create and configure Supabase storage buckets based on a configuration. It initializes an API client using `SUPABASE_PROJECT_ID` and `SUPABASE_SERVICE_ROLE_KEY` environment variables, then iterates through defined bucket configurations to create them.

```go
package main

import (
    "context"
    "fmt"
    "os"
    "github.com/supabase/cli/pkg/api"
    "github.com/supabase/cli/pkg/config"
    "github.com/supabase/cli/pkg/storage"
)

func main() {
    ctx := context.Background()

    // Load configuration
    cfg, err := config.Load("")
    if err != nil {
        panic(err)
    }

    // Initialize API client
    projectID := os.Getenv("SUPABASE_PROJECT_ID")
    serviceRoleKey := os.Getenv("SUPABASE_SERVICE_ROLE_KEY")

    apiURL := fmt.Sprintf("https://%s.supabase.co", projectID)
    storageClient := storage.NewClient(apiURL, serviceRoleKey)

    // Create buckets from configuration
    for bucketName, bucketConfig := range cfg.Storage.Buckets {
        bucket := storage.BucketConfig{
            Name:            bucketName,
            Public:          bucketConfig.Public,
            FileSizeLimit:   bucketConfig.FileSizeLimit,
            AllowedMimeTypes: bucketConfig.AllowedMimeTypes,
        }

        err := storageClient.CreateBucket(ctx, bucket)
        if err != nil {
            fmt.Printf("Error creating bucket %s: %v\n", bucketName, err)
            continue
        }

        fmt.Printf("Created bucket: %s\n", bucketName)
    }

    fmt.Println("Storage buckets seeded successfully")
}
```

--------------------------------

### Set Supabase Project Secrets

Source: https://context7.com/supabase/cli/llms.txt

Commands to set one or more secrets for a Supabase project using its reference ID. Supports setting individual secrets, multiple secrets at once, or loading from an `.env` file, with secrets immediately available to functions after deployment.

```bash
# Set single secret
supabase secrets set API_KEY=abc123 --project-ref zeoxvqpvpyrxygmmatng

# Set multiple secrets
supabase secrets set \
  DATABASE_URL=postgres://example.com/db \
  API_KEY=secret123 \
  --project-ref zeoxvqpvpyrxygmmatng

# Set from environment file
supabase secrets set --env-file .env.production --project-ref zeoxvqpvpyrxygmmatng

# .env.production content:
# STRIPE_SECRET_KEY=sk_live_xxx
# SENDGRID_API_KEY=SG.xxx
# OPENAI_API_KEY=sk-xxx

# Secrets are immediately available to functions after deployment
```

--------------------------------

### Monitor Supabase Database Replication using CLI

Source: https://context7.com/supabase/cli/llms.txt

These Supabase CLI commands enable monitoring of database replication slots and traffic patterns. Users can view active replication slots, their plugin type, and lag in bytes, as well as analyze overall traffic profiles for performance and synchronization insights.

```bash
supabase inspect db replication-slots --linked
```

```bash
supabase inspect db traffic-profile --linked
```

--------------------------------

### View Supabase Database Statistics using CLI

Source: https://context7.com/supabase/cli/llms.txt

This section provides commands to inspect various database statistics using the Supabase CLI. It allows users to check cache hit rates, sizes, WAL size, table statistics, index statistics, and vacuum statistics for their linked Supabase project. These commands help in monitoring database performance and health.

```bash
supabase inspect db db-stats --linked
```

```bash
supabase inspect db table-stats --linked
```

```bash
supabase inspect db index-stats --linked
```

```bash
supabase inspect db vacuum-stats --linked
```

--------------------------------

### Create New Supabase Database Migration (CLI)

Source: https://context7.com/supabase/cli/llms.txt

Generates a new empty migration file with a timestamp-based filename in the `supabase/migrations/` directory. This file is then edited manually to define database schema changes, such as creating tables and applying row-level security policies.

```bash
supabase migration new create_users_table

# Creates: supabase/migrations/20250124120000_create_users_table.sql

# Edit the file:
# CREATE TABLE users (
#   id uuid PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT gen_random_uuid(),
#   email text UNIQUE NOT NULL,
#   created_at timestamptz DEFAULT now()
# );
#
# ALTER TABLE users ENABLE ROW LEVEL SECURITY;
```

--------------------------------

### List Supabase Project Secrets

Source: https://context7.com/supabase/cli/llms.txt

Command to list all configured secrets for a Supabase project using its reference ID. Shows the name and value of each secret, indicating their availability as environment variables in Edge Functions.

```bash
# List secrets
supabase secrets list --project-ref zeoxvqpvpyrxygmmatng

# Output:
# | NAME                | VALUE           |
# |---------------------|-----------------|
# | DATABASE_URL        | postgres://...  |
# | STRIPE_SECRET_KEY   | sk_test_...     |
# | SENDGRID_API_KEY    | SG.xxx...       |

# Secrets are available as environment variables in Edge Functions
```
